revision.text:
[[ar:الإمبراطور أنكان]][[arz:الامبراطور انكان]][[azb:آنکان (ایمپیراتور)]][[cs:Ankan]][[de:Ankan]][[en:Emperor Ankan]][[es:Ankan Tennō]][[fa:امپراتور آنکان]][[fi:Ankan]][[fr:Ankan]][[hu:Ankan japán császár]][[id:Kaisar Ankan]][[it:Ankan]][[ja:安閑天皇]][[ka:ანკანი]][[ko:안칸 천황]][[mr:अंकन]][[nl:Ankan]][[pl:Ankan]][[pt:Ankan]][[ru:Император Анкан]][[sh:Car Ankan]][[simple:Emperor Ankan]][[sl:Cesar Ankan]][[sv:Ankan (kejsare)]][[th:จักรพรรดิอังกัง]][[tl:Emperador Ankan]][[tr:Ankan (imparator)]][[uk:Імператор Анкан]][[uz:Imperator Ankan]][[vi:Thiên hoàng An Nhàn]][[yo:Emperor Ankan]][[zh-min-nan:Ankan Thian-hông]][[zh-yue:安閑天皇]][[zh:安闲天皇]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Emperor Ankan.jpg|birth_date=0466|death_date={{death date|0536|1|25}}|birth_place={{ill|Kashihara|qid=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}|death_place={{ill|Kashihara|qid=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17|1=Japan}}|occupation={{ill|ruler|qid=Q1097498|1=ruler}}|title={{ill|Emperor of Japan|qid=Q208233|1=Emperor of Japan}}|spouse={{ill|Kasuga no Yamada no Himemiko|qid=Q11088803|1=Princess Kasuga no Yamada}}|father={{ill|Keitai|qid=Q349277|1=Emperor Keitai}}|mother={{ill|Owari no Mekohime|qid=Q106095999|1=Owari no Mekohime}}|relatives={{ill|Kinmei|qid=Q329792|1=Emperor Kinmei}}; {{ill|Senka|qid=Q329728|1=Emperor Senka}}|family={{ill|Imperial House of Japan|qid=Q909452|1=Imperial House of Japan}}|native_name=安閑天皇|resting_place={{ill|Kawachi Ōtsukayama Kofun|qid=Q11553391|1=Kawachi Ōtsukayama Kofun}}; {{ill|Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun|qid=Q11669285|1=Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun}}}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Emperor Ankan
{{nobold|{{lang|ja|安閑天皇|qq=}}|qq=}} | title = [[Okimi|Great King of Yamato]] | succession = {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|qid=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan|1=Emperor of Japan}} | image = | caption = | reign = c. 10 March 531 – 25 January 536 | coronation = | cor-type = Japan | predecessor = {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|qid=Q349277|lt=Keitai|1=Emperor Keitai}} | successor = {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|qid=Q329728|lt=Senka|1=Emperor Senka}} | posthumous name = [[Posthumous name#Chinese-style (Han-style) shigō|Chinese-style ''shigō'']]:
Emperor Ankan ({{lang|ja|安閑天皇|qq=}})

[[Posthumous name#Japanese-style shigō|Japanese-style ''shigō'']]:
Hirokuni-oshitakekanahi no Sumeramikoto ({{lang|ja|広国押武金日天皇|qq=}}) | spouse = [[Princess Kasuga no Yamada|Kasuga no Yamada]] | issue = {{plainlist| *Imperial Prince Ako *Prince Kibu *{{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|qid=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei|1=Emperor Kinmei}}|qq=}} | royal house = {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|qid=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan|1=Imperial House of Japan}} | father = {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|qid=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai|1=Emperor Keitai}} | mother = Menokohime | birth_name = Magari ({{lang|ja|勾|qq=}}) | birth_date = 466 | birth_place = | death_date = {{death date and age|536|1|25|466|qq=}} | death_place = | burial_place = {{Nihongo||古市高屋丘陵|''Furuchi no Takaya no oka no misasagi''|qq=}} (Osaka) ||qq=}} {{Nihongo|'''Emperor Ankan'''|安閑天皇|Ankan-tennō|qq=}} (466 – 25 January 536) was the 27th {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|qid=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan|1=Emperor of Japan}},{{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} (''Kunaichō''): [http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html 安閑天皇 (27)] according to the traditional {{ill|List of emperors of Japan|en|List of emperors of Japan|qid=Q188210|lt=order of succession|1=List of emperors of Japan}}.Varley, Paul. (1980). ''Jinnō Shōtōki'', p. 120; Titsingh, Isaac. (1834). {{Google books|18oNAAAAIAAJ|''Annales des empereurs du japon,'' p. 33.|page=33|qq=}} No firm dates can be assigned to this Emperor's life or reign, but he is conventionally considered to have reigned from 10 March 531 to 25 January 536.Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' p. 44. ==Legendary narrative== According to the ''[[Kojiki]]'', {{Nihongo|Prince Magari no Ōe|勾大兄皇子|qq=}}, later Emperor Ankan, was the elder son of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|qid=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai|1=Emperor Keitai}}, who is considered to have ruled the country during the early-6th century, though there is a paucity of information about him.Kelly, Charles F. [http://www.t-net.ne.jp/~keally/kofun.html "Kofun Culture,"] [http://www.t-net.ne.jp/~keally/index.htm Japanese Archaeology.] April 27, 2009. When Ankan was 66 years old, Keitai abdicated in favor of him. Ankan's contemporary title would not have been ''tennō'', as most historians believe this title was not introduced until the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|qid=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|qid=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō|1=Empress Jitō}}. Rather, it was presumably {{Nihongo|''Sumeramikoto'' or ''Amenoshita Shiroshimesu Ōkimi''|治天下大王|qq=}}, meaning "the great king who rules all under heaven". Alternatively, Ankan might have been referred to as {{Nihongo2|ヤマト大王/大君|qq=}} or the "Great King of Yamato". The most noteworthy event recorded during his reign was the construction of state granaries in large numbers throughout Japan, indicating the broad reach of imperial power at the time.Mason, Joseph. (2002). {{Google books|18oNAAAAIAAJ|''The Meaning of Shinto'', p. 172.|page=172|qq=}} [[File:Grave of Emperor Ankan.jpg|thumb|right|Memorial Shinto shrine and mausoleum honoring Emperor Ankan]] Ankan's grave is traditionally associated with the Takayatsukiyama [[kofun]] in [[:en:Habikino, Osaka|Habikino, Osaka]]. ==Genealogy== Empress: {{Nihongo|[[Princess Kasuga no Yamada]]|春日山田皇女|extra=d.539|qq=}}, {{ill|Emperor Ninken|en|Emperor Ninken|qid=Q357266|lt=Emperor Ninken|1=Emperor Ninken}}'s daughter Consort: {{Nihongo|Satehime|紗手媛|qq=}}, Kose no Ohito no Ōomi's daughter Consort: {{Nihongo|Kakarihime|香香有媛|qq=}}, Kose no Ohito no Ōomi's daughter Consort: {{Nihongo|Yakahime|宅媛|qq=}}, Mononobe no Itabi no Ōomuraji's daughter First son: Imperial Prince Ako. Second son: Prince Kibu Third son: Prince Akihinohohoshika, later Emperor Kinmei ==See also== * {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|qid=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan|1=Emperor of Japan}} * {{ill|List of emperors of Japan|en|List of emperors of Japan|qid=Q188210|lt=List of emperors of Japan|1=List of emperors of Japan}} * {{ill|Imperial cult|en|Imperial cult|qid=Q3271557|lt=Imperial cult|1=Imperial cult}} ==Notes== {{reflist|1|qq=}} ==References== * [[William George Aston|Aston, William George.]] (1896). [https://books.google.com/books?id=_oEfAAAAYAAJ ''Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697.''] London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trubner. {{OCLC|448337491|qq=}} * Brown, Delmer M. and Ichirō Ishida, eds. (1979). [https://books.google.com/books?id=w4f5FrmIJKIC&q=Gukansho ''Gukanshō: The Future and the Past.''] Berkeley: University of California Press. {{ISBN|978-0-520-03460-0|qq=}}; {{OCLC|251325323|qq=}} * {{ill|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|en|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|qid=Q3246771|lt=Ponsonby-Fane, Richard Arthur Brabazon|1=Richard Ponsonby-Fane}}. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|194887|qq=}} * {{ill|Isaac Titsingh|en|Isaac Titsingh|qid=Q332779|lt=Titsingh, Isaac.|1=Isaac Titsingh}} (1834). ''[[Nihon Ōdai Ichiran]]''; ou, [https://books.google.com/books?id=18oNAAAAIAAJ&q=nipon+o+dai+itsi+ran ''Annales des empereurs du Japon.''] Paris: Royal Asiatic Society, Oriental Translation Fund of Great Britain and Ireland. {{OCLC|5850691|qq=}} * [[H. Paul Varley|Varley, H. Paul.]] (1980). [https://books.google.com/books?id=tVv6OAAACAAJ ''Jinnō Shōtōki: A Chronicle of Gods and Sovereigns.''] New York: Columbia University Press. {{ISBN|978-0-231-04940-5|qq=}}; {{OCLC|59145842|qq=}} {{s-start|qq=}} {{s-reg|qq=}} {{s-bef|before={{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|qid=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai|1=Emperor Keitai}}|qq=}} {{s-ttl|title={{ill|List of emperors of Japan|en|List of emperors of Japan|qid=Q188210|lt=Emperor of Japan|1=List of emperors of Japan}}:
Ankan|years=531–536
''(traditional dates)''|qq=}} {{s-aft|after={{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|qid=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka|1=Emperor Senka}}|qq=}} {{s-end|qq=}} {{Emperors of Japan|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar|qq=}} [[en:Emperor Ankan]] [[ar:الإمبراطور أنكان]] [[arz:الامبراطور انكان]] [[azb:آنکان (ایمپیراتور)]] {{Commons category|Emperor Ankan}} [[cs:Ankan]] [[de:Ankan]] [[es:Ankan Tennō]] [[fa:امپراتور آنکان]] [[fi:Ankan]] [[fr:Ankan]] [[hu:Ankan japán császár]] [[id:Kaisar Ankan]] [[it:Ankan]] [[ja:安閑天皇]] [[ka:ანკანი]] [[ko:안칸 천황]] [[mr:अंकन]] [[nl:Ankan]] [[pl:Ankan]] [[pt:Ankan]] [[ru:Император Анкан]] [[sh:Car Ankan]] [[simple:Emperor Ankan]] [[sl:Cesar Ankan]] [[sv:Ankan (kejsare)]] [[th:จักรพรรดิอังกัง]] [[tl:Emperador Ankan]] [[tr:Ankan (imparator)]] [[uk:Імператор Анкан]] [[uz:Imperator Ankan]] [[vi:Thiên hoàng An Nhàn]] [[yo:Emperor Ankan]] [[zh:安闲天皇]] [[zh-min-nan:Ankan Thian-hông]] [[zh-yue:安閑天皇]] {{wikidata link|Q241765}} [[Category:6th-century Japanese monarchs]] [[Category:Emperors of Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]]
